title: Document and implement or remove remaining run/create CLI options
body: We still have unimplemented CLI options in `podman run` and `podman create` (for example, `--ip` and `--ip6`). We need to document what these are, and either implement them, or disable them and remove them from manpages.

comments.body:
Networking options we don't implement because of CNI:
- `--ip`
- `--ip6`
- `--network-alias`
- `--link-local-ip`

Attach-related options we don't support (could probably be implemented):
- `--attach`

Misc flags we don't have support for yet:
- `--volumes-from`

This is from a cursory scan of `podman run --help` - I'm sure there are others
@dcbw How close is CNI to implementing --ip and --ip6, that would seem to be a fairly common request?

Random fly by two cents:

I'd very much like to see `--volumes-from` supported.  When running something like mysql which has a VOLUME defined in the Dockerfile, to upgrade the container and not loose the state `--volumes-from` becomes important.

Regarding `ip`, `ip6`, `network-alias`, and `link-local-ip` I'd prefer you optimistically drop the options.  `ip` and `ip6` could maybe be a common ask, but today they only work in "user" (libnetwork) networks and not the default bridge.  So they aren't as commonly used even though the options exists today.

I tend to agree with dropping most of the network options.

Volumes-from should be doable, given we do share container create configs.
@umohnani8 Want to take a look at --volumesfrom?

regarding to `--ip` and `--ip6`

Can already be done by running `CNI_ARGS=IP=10.88.1.142 podman run ...` or `CNI_ARGS=IP=2001:0db8::42 podman run ...`

Unfortunately not possible specifying both at the same time
Doesn't look like the CNI API exposes the ability to set this outside of environment variables, which is somewhat disappointing.

Also, once the `--volumes-from` PR from @umohnani8 gets merged, I think we can close this, as the flags are removed or implemented. We should open a separate issue to track potentially supporting `--ip` (and poke at the CNI maintainers to ask if it's doable).
should we be filing an issue on CNI to make these exposed?
Yes, and closing this issue and making a new one focused on networking issues is a good idea.
